subject: Chemistry

question: Magnesium oxide is used in the making of the lining of blast furnaces. It is extracted from seawater as follows.  Aqueous calcium hydroxide is added to seawater. \(Ca{\left( {OH} \right)_2}_{\left( {aq} \right)} + MgC{l_2}_{\left( {aq} \right)}\) \to \(Mg{\left( {OH} \right)_2}_{\left( s \right)} + CaC{l_2}_{\left( {aq} \right)}\) The magnesium hydroxide is then filtered off and roasted. Which of the following comparisons between calcium and magnesium explains why magnesium hydroxide forms?
option1: Magnesium is less electropositive than calcium.
option2: Magnesium is lower than calcium in the reactivity series.
option3: The enthalpy changes of hydration for \(M{g^{2 + }}\) is less exothermic than for \(C{a_2} + {\left( {OH} \right)_2}\)
option4: The solubility product for \(Mg{\left( {OH} \right)_2}\) is lower than that for \(Ca{\left( {OH} \right)_2}\)
remarks: Correct answer is "The solubility product for \(Mg{\left( {OH} \right)_2}\) is lower than that for \(Ca{\left( {OH} \right)_2}\)"
